ukm:Metadata.dc:title: The Universal Credit Regulations 2013
ukm:Metadata.dc:subject: SOCIAL SECURITY
ukm:Metadata.dc:description: These Regulations contain provisions in relation to universal credit under Part 1 of the Welfare Reform Act 2012 (c.5) (“the Act”). They also include provision for a benefit cap under section 96 of the Act.

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: This amendment not applied to legislation.gov.uk. The table of contents for this legislation will be dynamically updated when its provisions are revised

Universal Credit Act 2025

ukm:InForce.OtherQualification: with effect in relation to assessment periods commencing on or after that date

ukm:UnappliedEffect.ukm:AffectingTitle: The Social Security (Income and Capital) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2020

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: This amendment not applied to legislation.gov.uk. The table of contents for this legislation will be dynamically updated when its provisions are revised
ukm:UnappliedEffect.ukm:AffectingTitle: The Social Security (Income and Capital) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2020
The Universal Credit and Employment and Support Allowance (Rates of Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
The Universal Credit and Employment and Support Allowance (Rates of Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 2026

Text.Addition: The relevant amount is determined by dividing the applicable annual limit by 12.
for a single claimant resident in Greater London who is not responsible for a child or qualifying young person;
joint claimants where either joint claimant is resident in Greater London;
a single claimant resident in Greater London who is responsible for a child or qualifying young person;
for a single claimant not resident in Greater London who is not responsible for a child or qualifying young person;
joint claimants not resident in Greater London;
a single claimant not resident in Greater London who is responsible for a child or qualifying young person.
For the purposes of section 96 of the Act (benefit cap) and this regulation a claimant is resident in Greater London if—
where the housing costs element is included in the claimant’s award of universal credit—
accommodation in respect of which the claimant meets the occupation condition is in Greater London; or
the claimant is in receipt of housing benefit in respect of a dwelling (which has the meaning given in section 137 of the Contributions and Benefits Act) in Greater London;
where the housing costs element is not included in the claimant’s award of universal credit—
accommodation that the claimant normally occupies as their home is in Greater London; or
where there is no accommodation that the claimant normally occupies as their home, the Jobcentre Plus office to which the Secretary of State has allocated their claim is in Greater London.

Para.Text: Sum in Reg. 80A(2)(c) substituted (1.4.2023) by The Benefit Cap (Annual Limit) (Amendment) Regulations 2023 (S.I. 2023/335), regs. 1(2), 3(c) (with reg. 1(3))
Sum in Reg. 80A(2)(d) substituted (1.4.2023) by The Benefit Cap (Annual Limit) (Amendment) Regulations 2023 (S.I. 2023/335), regs. 1(2), 3(d) (with reg. 1(3))
Sum in Reg. 80A(2)(a) substituted (1.4.2023) by The Benefit Cap (Annual Limit) (Amendment) Regulations 2023 (S.I. 2023/335), regs. 1(2), 3(a) (with reg. 1(3))
Sum in Reg. 80A(2)(b) substituted (1.4.2023) by The Benefit Cap (Annual Limit) (Amendment) Regulations 2023 (S.I. 2023/335), regs. 1(2), 3(b) (with reg. 1(3))

Reg. 80A inserted (with effect in accordance with reg. 1(2) of the amending S.I.) by The Benefit Cap (Housing Benefit and Universal Credit) (Amendment) Regulations 2016 (S.I. 2016/909), regs. 1(1), 3(4)
